reconciliations.target: Session D — cross-registry analysis (abomination + new covenant transformation)
reconciliations.rationale: Content concerns the abomination cluster's verse evidence and new covenant transformation as remedy for abomination. Not M01 content — no fear terms involved. Session D cross-registry defilement-redemption analysis is the appropriate scope.

reconciliations.target: Session D — anger cluster Session B analysis
reconciliations.rationale: Content concerns anger governance vocabulary in registry 4 (anger mastery as distinct inner-being sub-theme). No M01 terms involved. Belongs to anger cluster Session B analysis.

reconciliations.target: Session D — anger cluster Session B analysis; cross-cluster compound affective states
reconciliations.rationale: Content concerns compound anger-with-grief as a distinct inner-being mode in registry 4 (anger). No M01 terms involved. Belongs to anger cluster Session B and Session D cross-cluster affective-states synthesis.

reconciliations.target: Session D — divine pathos / C05 cross-cluster analysis
reconciliations.rationale: Content concerns divine pathos (God moved by misery of his people) in registry 5 (anguish). Not M01 content. Session D cross-cluster analysis of inner-being characteristics attributed to God is the appropriate scope.

reconciliations.target: Session D — anxiety cluster Session B analysis
reconciliations.rationale: Content concerns merimnaō (G3309) dual register in registry 7 (anxiety). merimnaō is not an M01 term. Belongs to anxiety cluster Session B analysis.

The finding asserts that the deepest fear/awe content integrates affective intensity and God-ward orientation inseparably. Phase 9 confirms this: T1.8.1 [A] identifies M01-A's primary dimension as affective-relational with multi-dimensional secondaries; T1.8.3 [A] confirms integration rather than competition between dimensions; T3.4.1 [A] confirms fear paired with joy and delight (Psa 2:11; Heb 12:28); T0.3.1 [A] establishes the God-ward orientation as constitutive of M01-A's image-bearing function. The finding's substance is captured.

reconciliations.target: Session D — brokenness cluster Session B analysis
reconciliations.rationale: Content concerns the brokenness registry (she.ver vocabulary) as a coherent inner-being condition. No M01 terms involved. Belongs to brokenness cluster Session B analysis.

reconciliations.target: Session D — distress cluster Session B analysis
reconciliations.rationale: Content concerns H7379 riv (strife/contention) group structure in registry 51 (distress). Not M01 content. Belongs to distress cluster Session B.

reconciliations.target: Session D — spirit-soul boundary / demonological-psychological interface analysis
reconciliations.rationale: Content concerns H7451A ra (harmful spirit afflicting Saul) in registry 51 (distress) — the demonological-psychological-theological boundary question. Not M01 content. Belongs to Session D spirit-soul boundary analysis.

reconciliations.target: Session D — distress cluster Session B analysis (vocabulary scope note)
reconciliations.rationale: Registry-level analytical note about outlier groups in registry 51 (distress) from XREF terms. Not M01 content. Belongs to distress cluster Session B analyst.

The finding identifies fobeō's tripartite inner-being structure: theophanic fear to worship (M01-B-VCG-01/02), reverential life-shaping fear of God (M01-A), and practical self-interested fear (M01-B-VCG-03/04). Phase 9 covers all three: T1.4.2 [A, B, CLUSTER] documents object-dependent operation across all registers; T7.1.3 [A] confirms the semantic range spanning reverential awe to cringing alarm; T1.2.1 [A, B] distinguishes dispositional from episodic forms corresponding to the tripartite structure. The finding's analytical insight is captured and confirmed.

The finding identifies the divine 'Fear not' command pattern (ya.re H3372G group 298-001) as a distinct inner-being structuring element — divine presence declaration plus commanded displacement of fear. Phase 9 addresses this directly: T1.4.1 [B] names commanded release as a mode of M01-B's operation; T5.2.1 [B] describes the before-during-after sequence; T5.3.1 [B] identifies divine word as the operative mechanism; T4.1.1 [B] documents God commanding the release of fear. The entire M01-B-VCG-06 (anchor Isa 43:1) is the dedicated Phase 9 finding for this pattern.

reconciliations.target: Session D — wonder/astonishment cluster Session B analysis
reconciliations.rationale: Content concerns wonder vocabulary (registry 175) as the inner state at the human-divine interface. Wonder terms (ta.mah, thambos) are in M01-BOUNDARY, noted in Phase 9 BOUNDARY characterisation for possible routing to a separate astonishment/wonder cluster. The finding's analytical scope belongs to that cluster's Session B. CARRY-TO-SESSION-D to allow the wonder/astonishment cluster to take up this question.

reconciliations.label: DIM-001-SD001
reconciliations.target: Session D — programme-wide pattern: redemptive vocabulary in moral-failure registries via XREF
reconciliations.rationale: SD_POINTER by designation. The pointer's question — whether redemptive vocabulary in moral-failure registries is a programme-wide pattern — is a Session D cross-cluster synthesis question. M01 is not involved in the abomination-debauchery pattern named in the flag.

reconciliations.label: PH2-004-001

reconciliations.rationale: Flag asks for cross-registry analysis of H7307J ruach/temper (anger governance at spirit level) between anger (reg 4) and spirit (reg 184). Not M01 scope. Session D anger-spirit boundary analysis is appropriate.

reconciliations.target: Session D — anger/abomination cross-registry boundary (za.am family)
reconciliations.rationale: Flag asks for cross-registry analysis of za.am family boundary between anger (reg 4) and abomination (reg 1). Not M01 scope.

reconciliations.label: PH2-004-003
reconciliations.target: Session D — NT orgē / eschatological wrath theology cross-registry analysis
reconciliations.rationale: Flag asks for cross-registry analysis of NT orgē as eschatological divine wrath connecting to justice, judgment, atonement, and soul vocabulary. Multi-cluster Session D theological depth question. Not M01 scope.

reconciliations.label: PH2-004-004
reconciliations.target: Session D — anger cluster data completeness (G3709 orgē)
reconciliations.rationale: Data breadth note for G3709 orgē in anger registry — Pauline wrath theology and Revelation sequences need additional verse coverage. Anger cluster data completeness note. Not M01 scope.

reconciliations.label: PH2-004-005
reconciliations.target: No replacing cluster_finding — data error corrected within source registry; no M01 characteristic involved.
reconciliations.rationale: The finding records a data error: H7110B (qe.tseph: splinter) was erroneously sharing a verse count with H7110A (qe.tseph: wrath). The finding text states H7110B has been assigned delete status in MTI, so the corrective action is already complete. Superseded by the completed correction — no ongoing analytical question, no M01 finding to replace.

reconciliations.label: PH2-004-006
reconciliations.target: Session D — relational vocabulary study (G3948 paroxusmos dual valence)
reconciliations.rationale: Flag notes the analytically interesting dual valence of G3948 paroxusmos (conflict vs. catalysis for love and good deeds) as relevant to Session D's relational vocabulary study. Anger/relational boundary question for Session D. Not M01 scope.

reconciliations.label: PH2-007-001
reconciliations.target: Session D — anxiety cluster data completeness (G4329 prosdokia)
reconciliations.rationale: Data breadth note for G4329 prosdokia in anxiety registry. While Luk 21:26 (prosdokia context) is in M01-B-VCG-03, that verse is routed under G4422 ptoeō (an M01 term), not G4329 prosdokia. The breadth note concerns prosdokia's adequacy in the anxiety cluster — not M01 scope.

reconciliations.label: PH2-007-002
reconciliations.target: Session D — anxiety cluster data completeness (G4328 prosdokaō)
reconciliations.rationale: Data breadth note for G4328 prosdokaō in anxiety registry — anxiety content is contextual not lexical, corpus thin. Anxiety cluster data completeness note. No M01 terms involved.

reconciliations.label: DIM-11-SD001
reconciliations.target: Session D — commanded-inner-state cross-cluster pattern (registries 11, 97, 42 and others)
reconciliations.rationale: SD_POINTER by designation. M01's contribution to the commanded-inner-state pattern is fully in Phase 9 (T1.4.1 [B], T5.3.1 [B], T4.1.1 [A, B]). The cross-cluster synthesis — tracing the pattern across registries 97, 42, and beyond — is Session D's scope.

reconciliations.label: DIM-61-SD001
reconciliations.target: Session D — tripartite dimension pattern cross-cluster analysis (orientation/affect/will-collapse)
reconciliations.rationale: SD_POINTER by designation. M01's tripartite pattern is confirmed in Phase 9: T1.8.1 [CLUSTER] (affective primary, volitional most consistent secondary), T3.6.1 [CLUSTER] (volitional faculty as primary operating theatre — will-collapse confirmed), T3.4.1 [CLUSTER] (affective dimension constitutive). Session D cross-cluster synthesis of whether other negative inner states share this structure is the appropriate scope.
